notes: This manifest records the Noise runtime approval boundary. Maverick has selected Snow 0.10.0 as a candidate implementation, records implementation-backed transcript evidence, includes a feature-gated core runtime session harness, keeps product transport config disabled, and makes no security-release claim.

runtime_gates.notes: Snow 0.10.0 is selected as the candidate standard Noise implementation for vector generation and the feature-gated core session harness. This does not expose Noise as a product transport or make a security claim.
The Noise vector file now includes a deterministic Snow-backed XX25519/ChaChaPoly/SHA256 H2 handshake transcript and transport smoke case.
The deterministic prologue context is mixed into a Snow-generated Noise XX transcript and checked against the committed vector.
Runtime config continues to reject Noise even when experimental settings are requested, keeping TLS 1.3 as the only accepted runtime suite.

The feature-gated core Noise session harness performs XX handshake, expected-remote-static checks, prologue/transport-context binding, length-prefixed encrypted message envelopes, and encrypted Maverick frame round trips.
Product config exposure remains deferred. advanced.crypto continues to reject Noise as an ordinary user transport suite until a separate product decision deliberately exposes it.

Formal human crypto review is not an active development blocker for this personal as-is prototype. Community or independent review remains required before any reviewed, stable, production, or censorship-resistance claim.
